title: AUC-preserving sigmoid/beta stack instead of isotonic final calibration
summary: For ROC AUC, monotone transforms are safe but isotonic can introduce ties and alter ranking; scikit-learn notes this explicitly.
rationale: For ROC AUC, monotone transforms are safe but isotonic can introduce ties and alter ranking; scikit-learn notes this explicitly. Beta calibration literature shows sigmoid can be misspecified, while beta calibration is a stronger parametric alternative. That makes the best low-risk ensemble path a logits-only stack with strictly monotone final calibration.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 diverse OOF base learners, such as the current feature-heavy GBDT, a native-categorical CatBoost variant, and a lighter model on mostly raw features. Stack their OOF logits with a tiny logistic or beta-calibration layer; avoid isotonic in the final submission path if leaderboard metric is pure AUC. Compare raw blend vs sigmoid vs beta on OOF AUC and tie counts.
expected_effect: Can harvest complementary errors from model diversity without sacrificing ranking quality at the final calibration stage.
risk: If base models are too correlated, stacking adds variance for negligible gain. Beta calibration may require a custom implementation.
